weather_report:
Perth settles into a damp, whispering night. Light rain drifts through the small hours, softening streetlights and dulling the edges of things; the sky feels low, a pocket of charcoal and pewter. Sunshine is absent now and will be shy at first light, with the UV essentially asleep until any breaks appear later. Expect a muted, hazy dawn with the patter easing at times, the air smelling clean and a little sea-salted, clarity restrained by lingering cloud.

It looks gently unpleasant for lingering outdoors yet oddly comforting for small, ordinary rituals. A light waterproof, closed shoes, and a compact umbrella will suit the early start; swap to a breathable layer if the clouds thin later, when the UV may rise from low to something modest. Good hours for unhurried tasks: queue a podcast while tidying the kitchen, write an overdue note, prep tomorrow’s lunch, or take a short, reflective walk between showers if you don’t mind the drizzle. Commuters should leave a touch early; cyclists, check the lights and fenders.
